events.title: Air quality warnings remain in place across US as wildfire smoke continues to swathe country
events.description: Rain could alleviate conditions in mid-Atlantic and north-east, with World Cup final expected to go ahead on Sunday Warnings of dangerous conditions are expected to remain in place on Saturday across swathes of the US, amid uncertainty about where the heavy wildfire smoke swirling from the Canadian province of Ontario and the US state of Minnesota will head next. Some parts of the US mid-Atlantic and north-east regions will continue to endure poor air quality until Saturday afternoon, where there is a high chance of thunderstorms , which could bring some reprieve from the poor air but come with other risks like flash flooding and high winds. Meanwhile, parts of the midwest and Great Lakes regions will continue to see dangerous air quality. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Air quality warnings remain in place across US as wildfire smoke continues to swathe country

events.title: Europe: Extreme weather fuels climate concerns
events.description: Europe is the world's fastest-warming continent, and this summer has already brought record-breaking temperatures, devastating wildfires, hailstorms and tornadoes. While climate change is not responsible for every extreme weather event, scientists say it is making such events more frequent and more intense. As the impacts continue to grow, the outlook for the future is increasingly concerning. Julitte Brown reports.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Europe: Extreme weather fuels climate concerns

events.title: Flash flood warnings strike north-east US amid wildfire smoke from Canada
events.description: Poor air quality from wildfires continues to plague New York City as several neighborhoods hit by thunderstorms Saturday is delivering a double dose of dangerous weather across the north-eastern US, with smoky skies from Canadian wildfires giving way to severe thunderstorms that have already triggered a flash flood warning as torrential rain pounds parts of the region. After a brief break on Friday, smoke from the wildfires moved back into New York City and surrounding communities on Saturday, sending air quality back into unhealthy levels . Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Flash flood warnings strike north-east US amid wildfire smoke from Canada

events.title: 5.6 magnitude earthquake hits Peru
events.description: Footage shows firefighters looking for a missing person amongst the rubble of a collapsed building in Pumpunya, Peru.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ 5.6 magnitude earthquake hits Peru

events.title: Russia hits Kyiv with ballistic missiles
events.description: Russia has launched one of its largest ballistic missile attacks of the conflict in Ukraine. At least one person was killed and 16 others were injured after around two dozen ballistic missiles struck Kyiv overnight. Witnesses described charred apartment buildings with windows blown out, mangled debris, and overturned cars scattered across the city. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said six districts of the capital were hit. France 24 correspondent Gulliver Cragg reports from Kyiv.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russia hits Kyiv with ballistic missiles

events.title: Kuwait, Bahrain respond to Iranian attacks amid growing escalation
events.description: The risk of a wider war in the Middle East is growing after authorities in Bahrain and Kuwait said they intercepted missiles and drones launched from Iran. Air raid sirens also sounded in Jordan's capital, Amman, while Israel said it detected Iranian missiles targeting the coastal city of Aqaba. The escalation comes after the US Embassy in Jordan urged American citizens, in a social media post, to avoid Aqaba's airport and seaport because of a specific and credible threat. Teaching Fellow in US politics and international security at University of Portsmouth, Dafydd Townley, shares his insights.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Kuwait, Bahrain respond to Iranian attacks amid growing escalation

events.title: Dozens missing after passenger ferry sinks off coast of Guyana
events.description: Emergency response has rescued 67 people, including 15 children, from one of the country’s worst maritime disasters A ferry carrying 116 passengers and crew sank ⁠off the coast of Guyana in South America ⁠late Saturday, with 67 ⁠people, ​including 15 children, rescued so far, according to Guyana ⁠authorities. A massive search-and-rescue operation continues for dozens of missing passengers in what has been described by officials as one of the country’s worst maritime disasters. Continue reading...

events.title: Djibouti: Emergency alert spotlights sharp hunger spike
events.description: More than 256,000 people in Djibouti – around 25 per cent of the population – are likely to face crisis or emergency level hunger over the coming months, a UN-backed global food monitoring network warned in a new report released Friday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Djibouti: Emergency alert spotlights sharp hunger spike

events.title: Venezuela: IOM warns of potential El Niño threat to families displaced by earthquake
events.description: Thousands of people remain displaced three weeks after the devastating earthquakes in Venezuela and the possible formation of a strong El Niño climate pattern could worsen their already dire situation, the International Organization for Migration (IOM) warned on Thursday.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Venezuela: IOM warns of potential El Niño threat to families displaced by earthquake

events.title: 8 killed and more than 60 wounded in Ukrainian drone attack on Russian regions

events.title: US soldier killed and one injured after Iranian attack in Iraq
events.description: The US military reports another death a day after two of its soldiers were killed in Jordan.

events.title: Russia's largest ballistic assault of war rocks Kyiv
events.description: Fires broke out across the Ukrainian capital after Russia's latest missile attack, which used the highest number of ballistic missiles of the war so far. Several people have been injured in Kyiv.

events.title: The week in pictures: The end of the World Cup, heatwaves and wildfires, war in Ukraine
events.description: The final stages of the World Cup, with Les Bleus’ elimination and England finishing third; extreme heat and wildfires around the world; and the Tour de France brought to a standstill by the heatwave in France. FRANCE 24 looks back at some of the week's most striking images.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ The week in pictures: The end of the World Cup, heatwaves and wildfires, war in Ukraine

events.title: UK aid cuts ‘reduce bilateral support to some African countries by 90%’
events.description: Critics say Foreign Office figures send ‘global message about the role the country wants to play on international stage’ Labour’s foreign aid cuts mean reductions of as much as 90% in the bilateral support the UK will give to some African countries, Foreign Office figures show. The department’s annual report includes a long-awaited breakdown of how the reduction in the aid budget will affect individual countries for the next three years. Continue reading...

events.title: Earthquake shakes Peru's Andes region, killing at least 5, displacing hundreds
events.description: A magnitude 5.5 earthquake near Sicaya killed at least five people and left hundreds homeless as adobe buildings collapsed across Peru's Andes region.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Earthquake shakes Peru's Andes region, killing at least 5, displacing hundreds

events.title: U.S. launches new airstrikes to 'punish' Iran for troop deaths
events.description: Three U.S. service members were killed in action while "partner forces defended against Iranian ballistic missile and drone attacks," U.S. Central Command said Saturday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 U.S. launches new airstrikes to 'punish' Iran for troop deaths

events.description: The number of injured remains unchanged at 16,740, government says.

events.title: Earthquake in Peru leaves at least six dead
events.description: More than 30 injured and 300 displaced after buildings collapse near Sicaya A 5.5-magnitude earthquake shook the Andes region of Peru, killing at least six people, authorities said Sunday. More than 30 people were injured and 300 have been displaced. The US Geological Survey reported the quake struck on Saturday at 9.24pm, with its epicentre located 2km (1.2 miles) west-south-west of the city of Sicaya in Huancayo province. It was 10km (6.2 miles) deep. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Earthquake in Peru leaves at least six dead

events.title: World Cup Final: La Roja, La Albiceleste battle for glory
events.description: La Roja's patient possession game meets the Albiceleste's trademark resilience in the World Cup final in New York on Sunday. As Spain coach Luis de la Fuente and his former coaching student Lionel Scaloni, now Argentina's manager, prepare to face off on football's biggest stage, the showdown promises a clash between the tournament's most polished side and the defending champions who have made a habit of surviving against the odds. France 24 jounalist Selina Sykes reports the latest from New York and France 24 International Affairs Editor Kethevane Gorjestani shares her insights.

events.title: Gulf crisis: Guterres calls for de-escalation, warns against return to full-on war
events.description: UN Secretary-General António Guterres voiced deep concern on Wednesday over the continuing military escalation in the Middle East amid ongoing strikes by the United States and Iran over control of the Strait of Hormuz.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Gulf crisis: Guterres calls for de-escalation, warns against return to full-on war

events.title: Security Council LIVE: International Criminal Court briefs on Darfur
events.description: On Wednesday, the Security Council heard an update on the International Criminal Court's work in Sudan's Darfur region, referred to the Court back in 2005. Focus has recently turned to atrocities in the ongoing war between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF). The briefing was held as the ICC faces mounting pressure: this week, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio launched a campaign to “dismantle” the Court, which prosecutes individuals for genocide, war crimes, crimes against humanity and the crime of aggression. Follow full live coverage below.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: International Criminal Court briefs on Darfur

events.title: Uganda calls for travel restrictions to be lifted after last Ebola patient discharged
events.description: Country begins 42-day countdown to outbreak being declared officially over, as numbers continue to rise in neighbouring Democratic Republic of the Congo Uganda has started lobbying countries to lift Ebola-related travel restrictions after discharging its last confirmed Ebola patient from hospital. The discharge of a Congolese national from the Mulago national referral hospital’s isolation centre in Kampala on Thursday triggered the start of a 42-day countdown required by the World Health Organization before Uganda can officially be declared Ebola-free, provided no new infections are detected. Continue reading...

events.title: US announces death of another service member, Jordan summons Iranian envoy over attacks

It followed the killing of two US troops in Jordan late Friday. Jordan on Sunday summoned Iran's top envoy in Amman to protest Tehran's attacks on its territory as Iran targeted US allies in the region in retaliation for US strikes across Iran.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US announces death of another service member, Jordan summons Iranian envoy over attacks

events.title: Cuba edges toward breakdown as power cuts and US meddling push society to brink
events.description: As Cuba swelters under six-month oil blockade imposed by US, tempers are fraying and unrest is growing When Cuba’s national grid collapses, as it did for the third time in 10 days on Tuesday, a collective groan spreads across its cities and people wonder, again, whether the island’s antiquated electricity system may soon become unrecoverable. The 777-mile long Caribbean island of 9.5 million people has been sweltering under a six-month-long oil blockade imposed by the US, part of a pressure campaign to bring down its communist government. But the parlous state of Cuba’s infrastructure goes far further back. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Cuba edges toward breakdown as power cuts and US meddling push society to brink

events.title: Weather tracker: Thunderstorms strike across Europe amid record heatwave
events.description: Storms are typical during intense heat but this week’s have been extreme. Plus, deadly monsoon rains in Bangladesh Hailstones the size of golf balls have been seen in French villages as, on top of the exceptional European heatwave, thunderstorms have struck across parts of Europe. While thunderstorms are typical during and after a period of extreme heat, the storms across countries such as France, Germany and Poland have been particularly severe, bringing flooding, strong winds and heavy showers with large balls of hail. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Weather tracker: Thunderstorms strike across Europe amid record heatwave

Reporting from Israel, FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky explains that Israel was hit by sharpnel from the interception. While no one was injured, there was a "sense in Israel that the war is escalating".

events.title: Australia updates travel advice for Laos as charges announced over mass methanol poisoning
events.description: Distillery owner could face only three months in jail over alleged poisoning of Australian teenagers Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Australian government has increased its travel alert level for Laos, advising travellers to “exercise a high degree of caution” due to the risk of methanol poisoning. It comes as a distillery owner in Laos was charged after a mass methanol poisoning incident killed six foreign nationals, including the Australian backpackers Bianca Jones and Holly Morton-Bowles. Continue reading...

events.title: News live: PM says Hanson should not ‘talk Australia down’ overseas; private schools cry poor in new ad campaign
events.description: Albanese’s comments come as One Nation leader nears end of UK tour. Follow today’s news live The arrest unfolded in real time, behind a bank of television microphones at Perth’s busy Mirrabooka bus station. As Western Australia’s police commissioner, Col Blanch, spoke to reporters on Thursday 2 July, a camera mounted on a nearby marked police van had been quietly panning across the interchange. It can scan hundreds of faces a minute, instantly cross-referencing their profiles against a database of about 4,000 people: those with outstanding arrest warrants, registered child sexual abuse offenders and people reported missing. Continue reading...

events.title: Cuban dissident artist arrives in US after 5-years in prison
events.description: A hero's welcome for Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara as he arrived in Miami following his release from prison in Cuba. The dissident artist accepted exile after the United States granted him humanitarian parole. Otero Alcántara co-founded the San Isidro Movement, a collective of Havana-based artists, writers, and musicians that protests against the Cuban government. In 2021, he was arrested in Havana during anti-government demonstrations and was later sentenced to five years in prison on charges of public disorder, contempt, and disrespect for national symbols. Carla Colomé Santiago, a Cuban journalist based in Miami, shares her insights.

events.title: ‘We are preserving a tradition’: how Ghana’s sensationalist film posters became collectible art
events.description: Hand-painted works are often wildly unfaithful to the movies they portray – reinterpretations that sometimes resulted in threats, insults and even physical attacks from viewers who felt duped Sitting on his porch in Teshie near Accra, Heavy J dipped a brush into red oil paint and dabbed it carefully on to his canvas – a flour sack – adding blood to a knife being wielded by a man. Higher on the canvas, he had started on an outline of a skull. Heavy J was creating a poster, but not as you might have expected for a horror film. Instead, it was for the animated fairytale The Little Mermaid. The man with the knife was not a killer but the film’s kind-hearted prince, Eric. The skull was also unrelated to the story. “We add more to make people interested,” said Heavy J, whose real name is Jeaurs Affutu. Continue reading...

events.title: Ukraine drones target Russian warehouses and oil sites

events.title: São Tomé and Príncipe heads to polls in tense presidential election
events.description: Incumbent Carlos Vila Nova hopes to defeat his former party and secure second term as independent Voters in São Tomé and Príncipe go to the polls for a presidential election on Sunday as one of Africa’s least populous countries seeks to burnish its democratic credentials. According to the National Election Commission, about 142,000 people are registered to vote in the tiny African state’s elections, approximately 15% of whom live in the diaspora. Continue reading...

events.title: Josh Kerr of Britain breaks 27-year-old world record in the mile
events.description: Kerr announced his bid to break the world record months in advance, and pulled off the plan in London on Saturday.

events.title: Spain win World Cup for second time after beating Argentina 1-0
events.description: After a 16 year wait, Spain have claimed a second World Cup, with a 1-0 win over Argentina. Spain are now men’s and women’s world champions.

events.title: China firm seeks damages over state control of British Steel
events.description: The UK government took over British Steel last year to prevent the closure of the country's last primary steelmaking facility. China's Jingye Group, which bought the firm in 2020, claims it is owed compensation.

events.title: France's Mbappe sets World Cup scoring record with 22 goals
events.description: Kylian Mbappe has moved past Lionel Messi on the all-time World Cup goal-scoring record in a spectacular match for third place. England won the game 6-4.

events.title: Hungary's president signs amendment ending his own term
events.description: An ally of former PM Viktor Orban has signed a constitutional amendment ending his own term as head of state. Tamas Sulyok criticized the law, while lawmakers insist he has impeded national progress by staying in office.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Spain crowned champions after extra-time thriller against Argentina
events.description: No description available.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Spain crowned champions after extra-time thriller against Argentina
events.description: Spain were crowned World Cup champions for the second time in their history after defeating defending champions Argentina 1-0 in extra time at the MetLife Stadium in New Jersey. Ferran Torres' clinical finish in the extra period ended a tightly contested final, giving Luis de la Fuente's Roja their first World Cup triumph since 2010 and completing a historic Euro-World Cup double.

events.title: World Cup final: "It is going to be the best day of my life"
events.description: "It's going to be the best day of my life," one supporter said before heading to the World Cup final.

events.title: Spain-Argentina live: Extra time begins with Albiceleste down to 10 men
events.description: Spain and Argentina meet in the World Cup final at the MetLife Stadium in New Jersey, with both nations seeking to etch their names in football history. The European champions face the defending world champions in a clash of styles, experience, and ambition as the tournament's ultimate prize hangs in the balance. Follow our liveblog for minute-by-minute commentary.

events.title: Argentina vs Spain: Tactics, stars and off-pitch backlash
events.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup reaches its climax as Spain take on Argentina in a highly anticipated final. The match pits the defending champions against one of football's rising powers, with Lionel Messi's enduring legacy meeting the emergence of Spain's teenage star Lamine Yamal

events.title: Argentina and Spain brace for World Cup final: The atmosphere from Madrid
events.description: The final hours before the World Cup final, where Argentina and Spain are set to face off. Despite scorching temperatures topping 40 degrees Celsius across much of Spain, fans are undeterred. Bars, public squares and fan zones are already full, with excitement building ahead of kick-off. For more on the atmosphere in Madrid, here's France 24 correspondent Sarah Morris.

events.title: England stun France in goal fest for third place
events.description: England finish third after dominating in the first half. France managed to claw its way back into the game, but did not manage to find the win for Didier Deschamps' last match as French manager. Mbappé becomes the highest scoring player in world cup history, and Michael Olise matches Lionel Messi's all-time assist record. Meanwhile, Spain are hoping to get another star on their jersey, whilst Argentina is looking to achieve back-to-back world cup victories.

events.title: Josh Kerr breaks 27-year-old mile record in front of packed London stadium
events.description: British runner Josh Kerr has broken the 27-year-old world record for the mile in front of a sell-out crowd in London.

events.title: Hunger striker taken to hospital as police launch 'chilling crackdown'
events.description: Police have forcibly removed hunger striker Sonam Wangchuk from a student protest site in India.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Hunger striker taken to hospital as police launch 'chilling crackdown'

events.title: FIFA to hand World Cup winners US-style championship rings
events.description: The winners of Sunday's World Cup final between Argentina and Spain will receive a championship ring for the first time, FIFA said.

events.title: Mexico's president to attend World Cup final at Trump's invitation
events.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said late Friday she will attend Sunday’s World Cup final after an invitation from her U.S. counterpart, Donald Trump

events.title: A former Ferrari boss has a blunt message: Your commute is more dangerous than his old job
events.description: Jean Todt spent decades around the deadliest motorsport on Earth. He was a rally co‑driver, then ran Ferrari’s Formula One team, then became president of world motorsport’s governing body. In his early years, a driver dying at the track barely made news.

events.title: Hunger deepens for displaced families in Sudan’s El Obeid
events.description: UN humanitarians working in war-torn Sudan on Friday highlighted rapidly growing needs among the more than 100,000 displaced people now sheltering in camps in the city of El Obeid.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Hunger deepens for displaced families in Sudan’s El Obeid

events.title: New EU border system tripling time at passport control, airport boss says
events.description: Ryanair has also warned passengers travelling to Europe this summer to prepare for extended waits.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 New EU border system tripling time at passport control, airport boss says

events.title: One anti-war critic fined, another held as Russia clamps down on dissent
events.description: While Boris Nadezhdin is barred from running for parliament, blogger Ilya Remeslo is remanded in custody.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 One anti-war critic fined, another held as Russia clamps down on dissent

events.title: Russians turn to cash, putting more strain on slowing wartime economy
events.description: Russians have been hit by mobile internet shutdowns and more businesses are seeking to dodge tax after more than four years of war with Ukraine.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russians turn to cash, putting more strain on slowing wartime economy

events.title: The UK gets its seventh prime minister in 10 years
events.description: Incoming PM Andy Burnham will immediately face foreign policy challenges, including the conflicts in Ukraine and Iran.

events.title: Five passengers found drifting days after boat sinks in Indonesia
events.description: Five passengers, including a seven-year-old girl, were rescued from a passenger boat that sank in Indonesia.

events.title: Retail spending fell in March as consumers pull back
events.description: Spending at US retailers fell in March as consumers pulled back amid recessionary fears fueled by the banking crisis.

events.title: Biodiversity fears as human-bred hybrid fish integrate into Philippines lake
events.description: Escaped flowerhorn cichlids are causing concern for native species and about parasites capable of infecting humans Escaped ornamental aquarium fish have integrated into a local ecosystem in the Philippines, but scientists say they may be threatening the native biodiversity of the lake. Flowerhorn cichlids – human-bred hybrid fish prized for their bright-gold colour and prominent head humps – are believed to have escaped from breeding facilities into Lake Sampaloc, which sits in a volcanic crater, during a typhoon. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Biodiversity fears as human-bred hybrid fish integrate into Philippines lake

events.title: Why is it so hard for the U.S. to win wars?
events.description: U.S. presidents have promised short, decisive wars in Afghanistan, Iraq and Iran. All have proved much more difficult than advertised and fallen far short of the political goals set at the beginning.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Why is it so hard for the U.S. to win wars?

events.title: Germany news: Merz moots Cabinet reshuffle after Spahn exit
events.description: Chancellor Merz has said he might use the forced resignation of a senior ally as a chance to reshuffle his Cabinet. Meanwhile, a train security guard was critically injured in a clash with a passenger. DW has more.

events.title: Andrew Tate, brother arrested in Miami, UK seeks extradition
events.description: Andrew and Tristan Tate were taken into custody by the US Marshals Service in Miami on a sealed warrant. The UK has requested their extradition on charges that include trafficking and rape.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Andrew Tate, brother arrested in Miami, UK seeks extradition

events.title: France commemorates the Vel d’Hiv roundup after a Paris plaque honoring rescuers is vandalized
events.description: France has commemorated the Vel d’Hiv roundup, a mass arrest of Jews by French police in 1942

events.title: Nigerian children pay the price for the Iran war as malnutrition and poverty surge
events.description: Health workers in northern Nigeria say more children are relapsing into malnutrition and cite knock-on effects from the war in the Middle East
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Nigerian children pay the price for the Iran war as malnutrition and poverty surge

events.title: Russian missile attack on Kyiv exposes holes in air defenses
events.description: Russia has attacked Kyiv overnight with ballistic missiles and other weapons.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russian missile attack on Kyiv exposes holes in air defenses

events.title: Massive fire destroys 100 homes in Norway and forces hundreds to evacuate
events.description: A major fire in southern Norway destroyed more than 100 homes and forced hundreds of people to evacuate the area

events.title: Haiti: Signs of hope despite gang violence and political uncertainty
events.description: For millions of Haitians, daily life has become a struggle to stay safe, find enough food and simply move around their own neighbourhood.

events.title: Peacekeepers step up support as families return to south Lebanon
events.description: With violence in south Lebanon significantly decreased since mid-June, UN peacekeepers there have returned to core functions such as protecting humanitarian aid and restoring critical infrastructure as families continue to return home.

events.title: Lindsay Clancy’s husband could testify for her at trial over deaths of their three children
events.description: Lindsay Clancy alleges doctors misdiagnosed her bipolar disorder and prescribed antidepressants that worsened her postpartum psychosis before the tragedy.

events.title: Boston police investigate death at home owned by Rep Ayanna Pressley's husband
events.description: Boston police homicide unit is investigating a death at a Mattapan residence owned by Conan Harris, husband of Rep. Ayanna Pressley, D-Mass.

events.title: US Forest Service workers kidnapped, zip-tied for hours by father-son duo: California AG
events.description: A 15-hour standoff in the remote wilderness of Northern California ended peacefully early Friday morning as the FBI Hostage Rescue Team secured the release of two forest employees.
events.ticker_label: 💥 US Forest Service workers kidnapped, zip-tied for hours by father-son duo: California AG

events.title: OnlyFans model seeks dead boyfriend’s animal-cruelty warrant in self-defense murder trial: Docs
events.description: Defense lawyers claim earlier elevator footage tells a different story than prosecutors allege in the Courtney Clenney murder trial pretrial battle.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 OnlyFans model seeks dead boyfriend’s animal-cruelty warrant in self-defense murder trial: Docs

events.title: US service member killed in Iraq as troops recover unidentified remains in Jordan: CENTCOM
events.description: A U.S. service member was killed in Iraq while handling explosives from a downed Iranian drone as troops searching in Jordan recovered unidentified remains.
events.ticker_label: 💥 US service member killed in Iraq as troops recover unidentified remains in Jordan: CENTCOM

events.title: Incoming UK PM Andy Burnham rejects Thatcher-era policies, signals leftward shift
events.description: Andy Burnham condemned Margaret Thatcher's legacy and promised public ownership of utilities, housing and transport in his first Labour leader speech.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Incoming UK PM Andy Burnham rejects Thatcher-era policies, signals leftward shift

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
events.description: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

events.title: China ‘strongly dissatisfied’ with nationalisation of British Steel
events.description: Move dealt ‘severe blow to Chinese companies’ confidence in investing in the UK’, says Ministry of Commerce China’s government has said it is “strongly dissatisfied” with the decision to nationalise British Steel this week, 15 months after the UK government intervened to prevent the closure of its steelworks in Scunthorpe and the loss of 4,000 jobs. On Thursday, British Steel was brought under public ownership to protect “the future of steel production”, the government announced. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 China ‘strongly dissatisfied’ with nationalisation of British Steel

events.title: Government use of automated AI decision-making to be curbed under new Australian rules
events.description: New national plan is accompanied by Labor push for digital duty of care legislation Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The use of AI in automated decision-making by government departments and agencies will be subject to tough rules under a new national plan, expected to extend to consumer protections, workplace safety and privacy. As the Albanese government grapples with the rapid growth in the use of artificial intelligence and a boom in datacentre construction, senior ministers have begun work to draw up new rules to ensure safety is built into AI processes inside government, prioritising fairness, accuracy and transparency. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Government use of automated AI decision-making to be curbed under new Australian rules

events.title: Cuban artist and dissident exiled from country after 5 years in prison arrives in US
events.description: A famous Cuban dissident artist and musician, Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara, arrived in Miami on Saturday after being released from a five-year prison sentence on the condition that he leave his country.

events.title: Chinese company demands compensation from the UK over British Steel nationalization
events.description: Jingye Group, the former owner of British Steel, has demanded compensation from the U.K. government for investment losses after the company's nationalization last week

events.title: Haiti: Training the next generation to fight corruption
events.description: How do you tackle corruption in a country grappling with insecurity, weak institutions and widespread impunity? In Haiti, the Government and the United Nations are betting on the country's youth.

events.title: Federal employees can download TikTok on government devices after ByteDance's divestiture, DOJ says
events.description: A DOJ memo reversed the 2022 ban after ByteDance transferred TikTok's U.S. operations and user data to a majority American-owned joint venture.

events.title: Health officials identify source of US explosive diarrhoea outbreak
events.description: The US Food and Drug Administration says iceberg lettuce supplied by Taylor Farms and sold at Taco Bell and Walmart locations was the source of cyclosporiasis infections.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Health officials identify source of US explosive diarrhoea outbreak

events.title: Cuban dissident Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara goes into exile in US
events.description: He was arrested in 2021 during Cuba's largest anti-government protests in decades and spent five years ⁠in prison.

events.title: Indian activist on hunger strike for 20 days forcibly taken to hospital
events.description: Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke has now begun an indefinite fast in Sonam Wangchuk's place.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Indian activist on hunger strike for 20 days forcibly taken to hospital

events.title: Seven Americans quarantining at Kenya Ebola facility after US travel ban, says aid group
events.description: Aid workers are first known people to quarantine at facility, which sparked huge opposition in Kenya Seven American aid workers who had been in Congo to fight the Ebola outbreak are quarantining at a new isolation facility in ⁠Kenya after the US government introduced travel ⁠restrictions, the head of a US charity ​employing them told Reuters. The aid workers are the first known people to quarantine at the facility, which has sparked huge opposition in Kenya and is at the heart of a legal case in which a court has ordered the work to be ⁠suspended. Construction continued, however, according to US officials and satellite imagery reviewed by Reuters. Continue reading...

events.title: Cuban dissident artist arrives in US after 5 years in prison
events.description: Luis Manuel Otero Alcantara, a prominent Cuban artist and dissident, was detained in July 2021 as he attempted to join unprecedented mass protests across the communist island.

events.title: Olympic champ Evenepoel wins Tour de France 15th stage
events.description: Belgian Remco Evenepoel beat race leader Tadej ​Pogacar to the finish line to win stage 15 of the Tour de France on Sunday after Jonas ​Vingegaard, ‌who was second in the overall ⁠rankings, was forced to abandon following a crash.

events.title: Denmark's Jonas Vingegaard, in second place in Tour de France, withdraws after fall
events.description: Denmark's Jonas Vingegaard withdrew from the 2026 Tour de France on Sunday after crashing around 20km from the end of the 15th stage. More to follow.

events.title: Former China Development Bank president is under anti-corruption probe
events.description: China's top anti-graft watchdog has announced an investigation into a former president of China Development Bank

events.title: Iran executes a man convicted of killing a security force member during 2022 protests
events.description: Iranian authorities have executed a man convicted of killing a security force member during 2022 protests

events.title: More than 500 people feared dead in double Myanmar shipwreck tragedy
events.description: There is growing concern for at least 500 people believed to have been aboard two boats that reportedly capsized off Myanmar’s coast in recent days.

events.title: Hoffa family urges FBI Director Kash Patel to keep investigation open and 'name names'
events.description: The Hoffa family wants the FBI to solve and name names in the legendary labor leader's 1975 disappearance, even if those responsible are deceased.

events.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
events.description: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

events.title: Algeria seeks larger EU energy role amid regional tensions
events.description: Algeria and Germany agreed to deepen energy ties during President Abdelmadjid Tebboune's recent visit to Berlin. But Algiers is still grappling with tensions at home, over Western Sahara and instability in the Sahel.

events.title: Mali rebels ambush army convoy, killing or capturing scores of soldiers
events.description: Both the regional al-Qaida affiliate and separatist FLA claimed responsibility.

events.title: As aid dries up, countries pledge to close the $4 trillion development gap
events.description: UN Member States reaffirmed their commitment to the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) by adopting a declaration on Wednesday that they will implement reforms to help close the $4 trillion SDG financing gap.

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
events.description: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

events.title: ICE arrests human rights lawyer who fled Chinese crackdown
events.description: Arrest in Pennsylvania of Wu Shaoping, who is awaiting asylum decision, raises fears of deportation and persecution A Chinese human rights lawyer has been arrested by US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), raising concerns he could be deported to China where he would face persecution. Wu Shaoping fled China at the end of 2019 amid a crackdown on human rights lawyers. He travelled to the US on a tourist visa and made an asylum claim in 2020, for which he is still awaiting a decision. Continue reading...

events.title: Australia’s life dissatisfaction levels double as ‘wellbeing poverty’ reaches new highs
events.description: ABS data shows one in 10 people are not satisfied with life – but community cohesion and trust lift happiness scores Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Nearly 2.2 million Australians 15 years and older are living below the wellbeing poverty line, with the share of people reporting very low life satisfaction doubling over the past decade. In 2025, 9.7%, or about one in 10 Australians, rated their general satisfaction with life at four or below out of 10, according to Guardian Australia’s analysis of the Australian Bureau of Statistics’s (ABS) general social survey. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Australia’s life dissatisfaction levels double as ‘wellbeing poverty’ reaches new highs

events.title: Andrew and Tristan Tate arrested in Florida on charges of rape and sex trafficking
events.description: Influencer Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan have been arrested in Miami after British authorities issued 38 new charges against them, and said they are "seeking extradition" for the two men.

events.title: Israel clears way for crocodiles to stop prison breaks
events.description: Israel's environment minister has reclassified crocodiles so that they can now be used for security purposes. Far-right Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir reportedly wants them to surround a jail holding Hamas militants.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Israel clears way for crocodiles to stop prison breaks

events.title: Leaked Iran report finds record public anger as regime focuses on holding power
events.description: Iran's confidential report prepared for President Masoud Pezeshkian reveals majority support regime change or deep reform amid surging public anger over food prices and corruption.

events.title: Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
events.description: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

events.title: Segregation in Israel: Separate streets for men and women?
events.description: An Israeli city with a large ultra-Orthodox Jewish population wants to segregate men and women on certain streets. Israeli politicians have voted to allow university courses to do the same.

events.title: Eight-month expedition to study the impact of climate change
events.description: As Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey, an adaptation of Homer's epic poem, arrives in cinemas, a very different odyssey is about to begin. Next month, a team of scientists will travel to the remote Arctic town of Kirkenes in Norway to board the floating laboratory Tara Polar Station for an eight-month expedition. The mission will study the impact of climate change and pollution on the Arctic Ocean. For more, we're joined by Marcel Babin, Scientific Director of the Tara Polaris I expedition.

events.title: Tanzanian-flagged tanker from Yemen hijacked by suspected Somali pirates
events.description: A Tanzanian-flagged tanker was hijacked by suspected Somali pirates off Yemen’s coast, security sources and local officials familiar with the incident said

events.title: Tennessee woman arrested after allegedly trying to drown one of her children in backyard pool
events.description: Mikaylee Rae Beirne of Johnson City faces aggravated child abuse charges after witnesses say she forced a child underwater in a backyard pool.

events.title: Tate brothers arrested in US as further UK charges take total to 59
events.description: The Crown Prosecution Service says it has requested their extradition from the US, while their lawyer says they are innocent.

events.title: The 1975's Matty Healy marries model Gabbriette Bechtel in Los Angeles
events.description: The newlyweds were first linked in 2023 after Healy's brief relationship with Taylor Swift.

events.title: Neighbours actor Terence Donovan dies aged 90
events.description: His son, actor Jason Donovan, posted to social media that his father died on Saturday night peacefully in Melbourne, Australia
events.ticker_label: 💥 Neighbours actor Terence Donovan dies aged 90

events.title: Hungary's president agrees to stand down after parliament backs removal
events.description: Tamás Sulyok, seen as an Orbán loyalist, had until Saturday night to agree to end his term.

events.title: German politician resigns over surrogacy child controversy
events.description: Jens Spahn faced criticism over his use of a surrogate, a ban on which he previously backed.

events.title: Space start-up launches India’s first commercial rocket designed for orbit

events.title: White House defends Argentina team over Falklands banner
events.description: The comments could further fuel the row over the incident, which has seen Downing Street back calls for Fifa to investigate.

events.title: ‘Sacred unity’: Iran’s supreme leader calls for calm amid political divides
events.description: Decree comes after hardline backers of the Iranian government accused officials of treason for signing an MoU with US.

events.title: Lebanon’s President Joseph Aoun meets Rubio ahead of Trump talks
events.description: Aoun is expected to meet Donald Trump on Tuesday, as Beirut seeks US backing to end Israel-Hezbollah fighting.

events.title: What happened to the Jewish left?
events.description: Marc Lamont Hill speaks to Molly Crabapple about the Jewish Labour Bund, anti-Zionism and the Jewish left today.

events.description: Government says the shake-up will strengthen security and clear the way for reconstruction.

events.title: Still haven't filed your taxes? Here's what you need to know

events.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
events.description: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

events.title: 'A definitive backslide.' Inside fashion's worrying runway trend
events.description: Now that the Fall-Winter 2023 catwalks have been disassembled, it's clear one trend was more pervasive than any collective penchant for ruffles, pleated skirts or tailored coats.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: 'I was up to my waist down a hippo's throat.' He survived, and here's his advice

events.title: Relaxed entry rules make it easier than ever to visit this stunning Asian nation

events.title: Australian and NZ safety assurances about asbestos in play sand were based on supplier’s own assessment
events.description: Exclusive: ACCC updates some recall notices to say products ‘may cause risk to health’, contrary to previous claims Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Schools, retailers and government agencies assured parents that asbestos-contaminated play sand posed a low risk to their children’s health based on a supplier’s own assessment which was not independently verified, the Guardian can reveal. The now-contested public messaging spread quickly after a range of Educational Colours children’s sand products were pulled from shelves in November 2025 after samples were found to contain asbestos , which was followed by more than 20 similar recalls across different brands and retailers. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Australian and NZ safety assurances about asbestos in play sand were based on supplier’s own assessment

events.title: Victoria announces new social media ‘demasking’ powers for accounts accused of vilification

The Victorian premier, Jacinta Allan, announced a suite of social media reforms on Sunday, saying families needed new ways to protect their children online. Continue reading...

events.title: Who is Andy Burnham, the U.K.'s new prime minister?
events.description: On Monday, former Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham succeeds Keir Starmer as the United Kingdom's prime minister. Burnham's politics have been shaped by England's north-south class divide.

events.title: An Israeli paraglider tries to save migrating swifts that nest in the Western Wall
events.description: Swifts have nested in Jerusalem's Western Wall since biblical times. Now one Israeli paraglider is trying to save them.

events.title: New Bundesliga TV deal in US prioritizes reach over money
events.description: The Bundesliga has a new home on American screens after Germany's top football league left ESPN. The new deal is a sign of the times.

events.title: Fact check: Spotting AI-generated 'historical' images
events.description: DW Fact check selected three "historical" videos that aren't real — they're AI. Could you recognize the fake? Here's how to spot it — and why it matters.

events.title: EU ban on destroying unsold clothes takes effect
events.description: Under new rules to reduce waste, large companies across the European Union can no longer dispose of unsold clothes, accessories and footwear.

events.title: Russian army deserters in Germany: 'No choice but to run'
events.description: Almost all asylum applications by Russian deserters are being denied by German authorities. DW and Russian-language media outlet Astra spoke to one of the young men who fears he'll be sent back to die on the front lines.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Russian army deserters in Germany: 'No choice but to run'

events.title: Germany: CDU's Jens Spahn resigns amid surrogacy controversy
events.description: Surrogacy is not permitted in Germany and the conservative Christian Democratic Union (CDU) opposes its legalization. Spahn and his husband had their child through a surrogate mother in the US.

events.title: India bans march on parliament by ‘Cockroach’ activists, risking a standoff
events.description: Police in New Delhi on Sunday banned a planned march on the Indian parliament by supporters of the viral, opposition Cockroach Janata Party. The demonstration set for Monday, the start of parliament's monsoonsession, follows growing calls for the education minister's resignation over exam paper leaks and the forced removal of a hunger-striking activist to hospital.

events.title: 🔴 Denmark's Jonas Vingegaard, in second place in Tour de France, withdraws after fall
events.description: No description available.

events.title: 'Shameless' fraudster who stole £300k from vulnerable pensioner led away by police
events.description: This is the moment a "shameless" fraudster who stole £300,000 from a vulnerable pensioner is led away by police, after Sky News tracked her down to Tenerife.

events.title: What to know about the challenges Andy Burnham will face as UK prime minister
events.description: The U.K.'s Andy Burnham enters 10 Downing Street on Monday with enthusiasm behind him, but major challenges ahead

events.title: Hungary’s president signs a constitutional amendment ending his term
events.description: Hungary's president has signed a constitutional amendment ending his term, resolving a dispute with the new government

events.title: Brazil's top court denies request for Argentina's Milei to visit Bolsonaro under house arrest
events.description: A Brazilian Supreme Court justice has rejected a request from lawyers for former President Jair Bolsonaro for him to be visited under house arrest by Argentine President Javier Milei

events.title: Baristas behind bars: Thai prisoners brew a path back to daily life
events.description: It looks like a regular coffee shop, albeit set up in an old red bus. Stairs lead up to a serving hatch, with a menu of drink options. A selection of jaunty coffee-related messages like “My one and only love: Americano” and “Miss you a latte,” adorn the side of the bus.

events.title: Guterres: AI must be shaped by 'all of humanity', not a handful of powers
events.description: UN Secretary-General António Guterres on Friday urged governments and technology companies to work together to ensure artificial intelligence (AI) benefits all countries.

events.title: Breaking the silence: Zimbabwean youth leader calls for urgent investment in mental health
events.description: For Tanatswa Amanda Chikaura, mental health advocacy began with a personal loss: while studying psychology at the University of Zimbabwe, she learned that a fellow student had died by suicide.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Breaking the silence: Zimbabwean youth leader calls for urgent investment in mental health

events.title: Tipster charged with murder after police say anonymous call helped solve 30-year disappearance
events.description: Christopher Popps allegedly sent multiple anonymous tips confessing to the 1993 killing of Patrick Orville Dearth before troopers traced them back to him.

events.title: Alabama man charged with killing military veteran, wife had posted about them online for a decade
events.description: Old Facebook posts reveal Alabama double murder suspect George Birmingham saw victims Justin and Lena Elrod as close friends for years.=

events.title: Democrat lawmaker's 107 mph traffic stop brings bodycam battle front and center again
events.description: The sheriff's office involved in a New Hampshire lawmaker's 107 mph traffic stop lacks body cameras and dashboard cameras due to funding shortfalls.

events.title: Andrew, Tristan Tate arrested in Miami as UK seeks extradition on expanded rape, trafficking charges
events.description: Britain's CPS approved 59 total charges against Andrew and Tristan Tate, including rape and trafficking, spanning alleged crimes from 2010 to 2017.

events.title: Man who allegedly rode naked atop moving locomotive busted after bizarre train stunt: sheriff
events.description: Daniel Ray Ballance of Quartzsite, Arizona, allegedly broke into a Union Pacific locomotive and surfed naked on its roof as it moved through Colorado.

events.title: California man admits targeting 3 Subway restaurants for robberies in less than a week, FBI says
events.description: Surveillance footage showed Freddie Alexander Lopez robbing three Subway restaurants over a six-day period in late January and early February. He pleaded guilty to a robbery charge.

events.title: Florida man who allegedly lured women with dating app could have more victims: authorities
events.description: Statistics show that more than half of adults under 30 have used online dating platforms. Experts suggest following a few key rules to stay safe.

events.title: Retired LAPD detective says Scott Peterson deserves a new trial, claims police overlooked critical witnesses
events.description: Laci Peterson's mother Sharon Rocha dismisses claims of new evidence as Scott Peterson's legal team pushes for a new trial after a denied petition.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Retired LAPD detective says Scott Peterson deserves a new trial, claims police overlooked critical witnesses

events.title: Alabama mom charged with murder after toddler bound, left to suffocate for 12 hours: cops
events.description: Sierra Hadaway faces murder charges after 2-year-old Madilynn was found bound and face-down on a bed for over 12 hours in Lanett, Alabama.

events.title: 'America's Most Wanted' fugitive captured on sailboat off NJ coast after 20 years on the run
events.description: The former anesthesiologist featured on "America's Most Wanted" was living as "Richard Graydon" aboard a sailboat named The Silver Lining for decades.

events.title: Trump administration touts more than $1B in UN budget cuts as reform push gains momentum
events.description: The Trump administration says it secured over $1 billion in U.N. budget cuts in six months, including a $550 million peacekeeping budget reduction.

events.title: Asian nation with 1,500-year-old imperial line insists only men can become emperor in policy revision
events.description: Japan's Parliament revised the Imperial House Law to cement male-only succession, blocking Princess Aiko from inheriting the throne despite public support.

events.title: Resource-rich nation praises US ties amid Washington-Beijing critical minerals race
events.description: Foreign Minister Thérèse Kayikwamba Wagner says the Democratic Republic of the Congo needs multiple partners to develop its cobalt, copper and lithium wealth amid growing U.S. interest.

events.title: African growth boom follows Trump push to replace aid with trade
events.description: Despite predictions of collapse after USAID cuts, sub-Saharan Africa is outpacing Asia in growth forecasts, fueled by trade and private investment.

events.title: Who is Andy Burnham? The Trump critic set to become the UK's next prime minister
events.description: Andy Burnham secured 322 MP nominations on day one of the leadership process, but critics say he faces less scrutiny than any recent U.K. prime minister.

events.title: Trump officials unveil private sector blueprint for life after USAID
events.description: Ambassador Mike Waltz says the Trump administration is replacing traditional foreign aid with private investment and trade to create jobs, cut dependency.

events.title: FBI snares an American heir indicted for allegedly bankrolling anti-cop, pro-Hamas communist revolution
events.description: James Cox Chambers Jr., estranged heir to the $27 billion Cox empire, was detained in Spain for alleged money laundering and Hamas support.
